note: Block Visibility Groups exposes a `block_visibility_group` config entity, a `condition_group` block visibility plugin, a group evaluator service, and an admin UI under Block Layout. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) — they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which config entity type does Block Visibility Groups define to hold a reusable, named set of block visibility conditions?
evals.expected_output: The `block_visibility_group` config entity (class BlockVisibilityGroup) stores a group's label, logic, conditions and allow_other_conditions flag.

evals.prompt: How do I make a specific Drupal block use a block visibility group's conditions? Which visibility plugin on the block form is involved?
evals.expected_output: Edit the block's Visibility settings and pick the group in the Condition Group section, which is the `condition_group` visibility plugin the module adds to every block.

evals.prompt: Which condition plugins can I add to a Block Visibility Group, and is the CTools module required to use the module?
evals.expected_output: A group uses core's condition plugin manager, so any core or contrib condition plugin works. CTools is only a recommended module (it ships extra conditions); the module's sole dependency is core `block`.

evals.prompt: In a Block Visibility Group, which config key decides whether ALL conditions must pass versus only one, and what are its values?
evals.expected_output: The `logic` key on the group: `and` (all conditions must pass, the default) or `or` (only one condition must pass).

evals.prompt: What is the admin path (and route name) for managing block visibility groups on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: Manage them at /admin/structure/block/block-visibility-group (route entity.block_visibility_group.collection), shown under Structure → Block Layout.

evals.prompt: Which permission does a user need to create and manage block visibility groups through the UI on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: The core `administer blocks` permission gates all of the module's routes (the module also declares `administer block visibility groups` as the entity admin_permission).

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, which service evaluates whether a block visibility group's conditions currently pass, and which method do I call?
evals.expected_output: The `block_visibility_groups.group_evaluator` service (GroupEvaluator); call evaluateGroup($block_visibility_group) which returns a bool after applying contexts and resolving conditions with the group's logic.

evals.prompt: In Block Visibility Groups, what does the `allow_other_conditions` setting on a group do?
evals.expected_output: When off, blocks placed in the group have their per-block visibility options removed so the group is the only source of visibility rules; when on, blocks may add extra conditions on top of the group's.
